fault_hypothesis: The overlap condition on line 76 uses strict inequalities (`end <= s` and `start >= e`), which allows adjacent intervals (touching at endpoints) to be accepted. The condition should use strict inequalities in the other direction, i.e., `end < s or start > e` to treat touching as a conflict, or the logic should be `end < s or start > e` depending on the desired semantics — here the tests require that touching endpoints also count as overlap.

spec.expr: not (end__1 == s or e == start__1) or (not (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e))

constraints.intent: When new interval touches an existing one at exactly one endpoint, the condition should detect overlap (return False), but the current strict-inequality check fails to do so.
At each loop iteration, if the new interval is adjacent (touching) to an existing slot, the loop should have already returned False — so we should never reach the tail with a touching pair.
Confirm that end is strictly greater than start before using them in the overlap check.
After the non-overlap branch (no return), the new interval must be strictly non-adjacent to the current existing slot — touching endpoints should have triggered a return.
